Internet technologies will prove most valuable as catalysts to improve supply chain management — especially procurement — not consumer retail
notes: This was Aberdeen's distinctive thesis separating B2B from B2C value

metric_value: Procurement of non-production goods and services has been an overlooked opportunity for cost management at most organizations
notes: Indirect/MRO spending was systematically unmanaged in 1998

metric_value: Internet Procurement has potential to dramatically redesign and improve purchasing procedures with rapid and measurable process/cost/profit improvements
notes: Aberdeen's core prediction: e-procurement would deliver ROI

metric_value: Ariba acquired by SAP for $4.3B in 2012; validated as the dominant Internet procurement platform with 2.5M+ vendor network
notes: SAP Ariba processes $1.3T in annual commerce; vindicates Aberdeen's B2B procurement thesis

metric_value: Commerce One filed for bankruptcy October 2004; acquired by Perfect Commerce 2006; failed to sustain despite IPO success
notes: Commerce One's failure shows dot-com excesses even in validated B2B procurement space

metric_value: Early adopter survey of Internet Procurement automation users; product selection and actual benefits realized
notes: Survey focused on measurable outcomes from early adopters — a rigorous methodology for 1998

metric_value: Internet procurement became standard enterprise capability; SAP Ariba alone processes $1.3T annual commerce
notes: Aberdeen's market prediction proved highly accurate

metric_value: SAP positioned as ERP vendor beginning to integrate internet procurement capabilities; partnered with Commerce One December 1997
